images.title: Tables à huîtres à l'embouchure de la Seudre (Ifremer 00689-80156 - 32647).jpg
images.description: These oyster tables are one of the main farming techniques: raised, the oysters are installed in pockets placed on a structure (here, a table) and raised in parks on the foreshore. Oyster production occupies approximately <sup>1/3</sup> of the surface area of the Seudre marshes. This represents nearly <sub>1/3</sub> of the marketing of French oysters (FranceAgrimer <sub>,</sub> 2011), <sup>a</sup> large part of which benefits from the "Marennes-Oléron" label. These aerial images of oyster beds were taken with a drone, in La Tremblade, a town located in the Charente-Maritime department in the Nouvelle-Aquitaine region.

images.title: Station Ifremer de La Tremblade (Ifremer 00581-69313 - 28547).jpg
images.description: The Ifremer station at La Tremblade, shown here in aerial photography, specializes in the fields of shellfish farming, aquaculture and monitoring of the coastal environment.
